api/spec/packages/legacy/src/app.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the installed Apps API — marketplace listings, OAuth2/API-key install flows, Stripe, Sandbox, and CustomInvoicing app types, and per-customer app data. Every model here feeds api/openapi.yaml and the generated Go/JS/Python SDKs.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic app types
patterns.description: App, AppReplaceUpdate, and CustomerAppData are all `@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })` unions. Adding a new app type requires a new union variant in all three.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union App { stripe: StripeApp, sandbox: SandboxApp, custom_invoicing: CustomInvoicingApp }

patterns.name: AppBase spread for concrete app models
patterns.description: All concrete app models (StripeApp, SandboxApp, CustomInvoicingApp) spread `...AppBase` and add a literal `type: AppType.<Value>` field. Never duplicate AppBase fields.

patterns.name: CustomerAppBase generic for per-app customer data
patterns.description: Per-app customer data models spread `...CustomerAppBase<AppType.X>` to inherit common `id?` and `type` fields, then add provider-specific fields.

patterns.name: Interface-per-resource with @route, @tag, @operationId
patterns.description: Each HTTP resource is an `interface` decorated with `@route`, `@tag`, and operations use `@operationId` + `@summary`. Never write bare operations outside an interface.

patterns.name: main.tsp as the import manifest
patterns.description: main.tsp only imports other .tsp files; it contains no type definitions. It is the entry point imported by cloud/main.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: Defines AppsEndpoints interface, AppBase, App union, AppStatus enum, AppType enum, and AppReference. Core scaffolding for all app types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: App and AppReplaceUpdate unions must stay in sync — adding an app type to App without adding it to AppReplaceUpdate breaks PUT.

key_file_guides.role: MarketplaceEndpoints interface covering listing discovery, OAuth2 install, API-key install, and plain install. Defines MarketplaceListing, AppInstallMethod, and install request/response models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: installMethods on MarketplaceListing is informational only — actual install routes live in MarketplaceEndpoints.

key_file_guides.role: AppStripeEndpoints (webhook, deprecated updateStripeAPIKey, createCheckoutSession) plus all Stripe-specific checkout session option models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: updateStripeAPIKey is deprecated; new stripe config changes go through PUT /api/v1/apps/{id}.

key_file_guides.role: CustomInvoicingApp model and AppCustomInvoicingEndpoints for draft/issuing sync callbacks and payment status updates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomInvoicing uses async hooks (enableDraftSyncHook, enableIssuingSyncHook); consumers must POST to draftSynchronized/issuingSynchronized to progress invoice state.

key_file_guides.role: OAuth2 namespace with AuthorizationCodeGrantParams combining success and error query params into a single model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses `OAuth2` namespace, not `OpenMeter` — imports from this file must reference `OAuth2.ClientAppStartResponse` etc.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerAppData union and per-provider customer app data models (StripeCustomerAppData, SandboxCustomerAppData, CustomInvoicingCustomerAppData).
key_file_guides.watch_for: customer.tsp here is under the `app/` sub-folder and is separate from the `customer/` folder's customer.tsp.

anti_patterns: Adding app-type-specific fields directly to AppBase — each app type has its own concrete model.
Defining new endpoints outside a named interface with @route and @tag.
Adding a new AppType variant only to App union without updating AppReplaceUpdate and CustomerAppData.
Using `@body` on list/GET operations — paginated params use spread models like `...QueryPagination`.
Importing types from sibling app/*.tsp files without going through main.tsp at build time.

decisions.decision: Discriminated unions with envelope:none for App/AppReplaceUpdate/CustomerAppData
decisions.rationale: Allows a single OpenAPI oneOf with a flat discriminator property, matching client SDK code-gen expectations and the Go handler type-switch pattern.

decisions.decision: Separate AppBase model spread into each concrete app
decisions.rationale: Avoids deep inheritance chains that TypeSpec/oapi-codegen struggle with; each concrete model is self-contained for SDK generation.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new app type (e.g. PayPal)

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/cloud.purpose: Cloud-variant spec entry point that re-exports the entire OpenMeter API under the `OpenMeterCloud` namespace with cloud-specific auth schemes (BearerAuth + cookie + portal token) and Svix/cloud server URLs. Contains no new type definitions — only namespace interface extensions.

patterns.name: Interface extension via `extends`
patterns.description: Every interface in cloud/main.tsp extends an OpenMeter interface with a new `@route` and `@tag` override. Never copy-paste operations — always extend.

patterns.name: Separate OpenMeterCloud namespace blocks
patterns.description: Related interface groups are collected in separate `namespace OpenMeterCloud { ... }` blocks for readability. Multiple blocks with the same namespace name are valid TypeSpec.
patterns.example: namespace OpenMeterCloud { @route("/api/v1/apps") interface AppsEndpoints extends OpenMeter.AppsEndpoints {} }

patterns.name: Auth overrides at service level
patterns.description: auth.tsp defines CloudTokenAuth, CloudCookieAuth, CloudPortalTokenAuth. The `@useAuth(CloudTokenAuth | CloudCookieAuth)` decorator on the `@service` replaces the default OpenMeter self-hosted auth for the cloud spec.
patterns.example: @service(#{ title: "OpenMeter Cloud API" }) @useAuth(CloudTokenAuth | CloudCookieAuth) namespace OpenMeterCloud;

key_file_guides.role: Defines the three cloud auth schemes as models. No routes or operations here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CloudPortalTokenAuth is a BearerAuth; the portal meters endpoint separately applies `@useAuth(CloudPortalTokenAuth)` to scope portal-only access.

key_file_guides.role: The cloud OpenAPI spec entry point. Imports `..` (the main legacy spec), then auth.tsp, then declares OpenMeterCloud namespace with all interface extensions. All cloud-facing endpoint routing lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When adding a new endpoint group to the base OpenMeter namespace, add a corresponding `extends` interface here so it appears in the cloud OpenAPI output.

anti_patterns: Defining new models or type aliases in cloud/ — all types belong in the base OpenMeter namespace.
Duplicating operation definitions instead of using `extends`.
Adding auth decorators to individual operations in cloud/main.tsp — service-level `@useAuth` covers all.

decisions.decision: cloud/main.tsp extends rather than duplicates base OpenMeter interfaces
decisions.rationale: Single source of truth for operation contracts; cloud only differs in auth, servers, and tag metadata.

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/customer.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Customer API — CRUD, listing with filters, per-customer app data (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing), and a legacy Stripe convenience endpoint. Models feed api/openapi.yaml and SDKs; the Customer model is referenced throughout subscriptions, billing, and entitlements.

patterns.name: ResourceCreateModel / ResourceReplaceModel for mutation bodies
patterns.description: Create bodies use `TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel<Customer>` and update bodies use `TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceReplaceModel<Customer>`. Never inline field lists for create/update.

patterns.name: @visibility annotations control lifecycle exposure
patterns.description: Fields like `currentSubscriptionId`, `subscriptions`, and `annotations` carry `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` so they are excluded from create/update. Spread `...Resource` on entity models to inherit id/timestamps automatically.

patterns.name: Filter params as spread models in interface operations
patterns.description: List filter params (name, key, primaryEmail, subject, planKey) are declared as a `model ListCustomersParams` and spread into the list operation, keeping operation signatures short.

patterns.name: ULIDOrExternalKey path param for customer lookup
patterns.description: All per-customer endpoints use `@path customerIdOrKey: ULIDOrExternalKey` to support both ULID and string key lookup without duplicate routes.
patterns.example: @get @route("/{customerIdOrKey}") get(@path customerIdOrKey: ULIDOrExternalKey, ...GetCustomerParams): Customer | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;

key_file_guides.role: Defines Customer model, CustomersEndpoints interface, and all list/filter param models. Customer spreads `...Resource` and exposes `subscriptions` as a Lifecycle.Read-only expand.
key_file_guides.watch_for: `subscriptions` field is only populated when `expand=subscriptions` is passed; it is not part of default responses.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerAppsEndpoints interface for list/upsert/delete of CustomerAppData, and ListCustomerAppDataParams model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This app.tsp is under customer/ and defines customer-scoped app data endpoints, not the app-domain app.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: CustomerStripeEndpoints with get/upsert Stripe app data and createPortalSession. Stripe-specific convenience endpoints separate from the generic app data API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: createPortalSession returns 201, not 200 — matches the Go handler response encoding.

key_file_guides.role: Imports app.tsp, customer.tsp, stripe.tsp. No definitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new customer sub-resource .tsp files here.

anti_patterns: Adding billing-specific fields to the Customer model — billing overrides live in the billing/ sub-folder.
Hardcoding filter params inline in an operation instead of a spread model.
Using `@body` on GET list operations — list filters must be `@query` params.
Duplicating Stripe-specific customer fields in the generic CustomerAppData models.

decisions.decision: CustomerUsageAttribution as a nested model rather than flat subjectKeys array on Customer
decisions.rationale: Preserves room for future attribution dimensions (e.g. account-level attribution) without a breaking change.

decisions.decision: Stripe customer portal session in customer/stripe.tsp rather than app/stripe.tsp
decisions.rationale: The portal session is customer-scoped (requires customerIdOrKey path param), not app-scoped.

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/info.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for static lookup endpoints: supported currencies list and async operation progress tracking. Small self-contained sub-package; no mutable resources, no pagination on currencies.

patterns.name: Flat model for static lookup entities
patterns.description: Currency and Progress have no Resource spread (no id/timestamps) because they are not persisted domain entities with lifecycle. Currency returns a plain array, not a paginated response.

patterns.name: routes.tsp holds interface definitions, currencies.tsp holds models
patterns.description: Model types (Currency, Progress) live in their own files; routing interfaces live in routes.tsp or the model file. main.tsp imports all and re-exports.
patterns.example:
// routes.tsp imports ../rest.tsp and defines CurrenciesEndpoints
// currencies.tsp defines the Currency model only

key_file_guides.role: CurrenciesEndpoints interface at /api/v1/info/currencies. Single list endpoint returning Currency[].
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns a plain array, not PaginatedResponse — do not add pagination here without a spec change.

key_file_guides.role: ProgressEndpoints interface and Progress model. GET by string id, not ULID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Progress id is a plain `string`, not `ULID` — matches the progressmanager domain.

key_file_guides.role: Currency model with code, name, symbol, subunits. No routes here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: subunits is uint32; zero-subunit currencies (JPY) are valid.

anti_patterns: Adding paginated responses to the currencies list endpoint — it returns all currencies as a flat array.
Adding mutable (POST/PUT/DELETE) operations — this folder is read-only lookup data.

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/notificatio.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Notifications API — channels (webhook), rules (balance threshold, entitlement reset, invoice events), and event/delivery-status tracking. Discriminated unions on NotificationRule and NotificationChannel allow type-safe multi-variant webhook rules.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic rule and channel types
patterns.description: NotificationRule, NotificationRuleCreateRequest, NotificationChannel, and NotificationChannelCreateRequest are all `@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })` unions. Adding a rule type requires updating all four unions.

patterns.name: NotificationRuleCommon<T> generic for rule base fields
patterns.description: All rule models spread `...NotificationRuleCommon<NotificationEventType.X>` to inherit id, type (literal), name, disabled, channels, and metadata. Never duplicate these fields.

patterns.name: CreateRequest models use OmitProperties to strip read-only references
patterns.description: Rule create request models use `OmitProperties<NotificationRuleXxx, "channels" | "features">` then re-add `channels: Array<ULID>` (IDs only) and `features?: Array<ULIDOrKey>` for the writable form.
patterns.example: @withVisibility(Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) model NotificationRuleBalanceThresholdCreateRequest { ...OmitProperties<NotificationRuleBalanceThreshold, "channels" | "features">; channels: Array<ULID>; features?: Array<ULIDOrKey>; }

patterns.name: Payload models carry full denormalized context
patterns.description: NotificationEventBalanceThresholdPayload and NotificationEventResetPayload spread `...NotificationEventEntitlementValuePayloadBase` to embed entitlement, feature, subject, value, and customer inline — payloads are self-contained for webhook consumers.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationChannel/NotificationChannelWebhook models, NotificationChannelsEndpoints interface, NotificationChannelType enum. Includes CRUD for channels.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NotificationChannelType.webhook uses uppercase value `"WEBHOOK"` with a legacy `#suppress` annotation — preserve this for backward compat.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationRulesEndpoints interface, NotificationRule union, all rule variant models, and NotificationRuleCreateRequest union. Also has a `/{ruleId}/test` POST endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Rule update uses PUT with NotificationRuleCreateRequest (same schema as create) — not PATCH.

key_file_guides.role: Entitlement-specific rule and event payload models: NotificationRuleBalanceThreshold, NotificationRuleEntitlementReset, and their payload models. Also defines FeatureMeta.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NotificationRuleBalanceThresholdValueType has deprecated PERCENT/NUMBER variants — keep them for backward compat, add new variants alongside.

key_file_guides.role: Invoice event payload models (NotificationEventInvoicePayload) and invoice notification rules (NotificationRuleInvoiceCreated, NotificationRuleInvoiceUpdated).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Invoice notification rules reference billing Invoice models — ensure billing/ imports are present.

key_file_guides.role: NotificationEventType enum, NotificationEventDeliveryStatus/State models, and delivery attempt models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All NotificationEventType values use dotted lowercase (entitlements.balance.threshold) with `#suppress` for casing — maintain this pattern for new event types.

key_file_guides.role: Import manifest only. No definitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new .tsp files here when adding notification event families.

anti_patterns: Adding a new rule type only to NotificationRule union without also updating NotificationRuleCreateRequest.
Putting delivery status logic into channel.tsp — delivery status models belong in event.tsp.
Using PATCH for rule updates — the API uses PUT with the full create-request body.
Hardcoding feature/channel lists inline in rule models instead of using FeatureMeta and NotificationChannelMeta spread models.

decisions.decision: OmitProperties + re-add writable-form fields for create requests
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidental write of server-assigned fields (channel full objects, feature full objects) while keeping the shared model as the source of truth for read responses.

decisions.decision: Payload models embed full denormalized context (entitlement, feature, subject, customer)
decisions.rationale: Webhook consumers should not need a second API call to act on a notification — payloads are designed to be self-contained.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new notification rule type (e.g. subscription.cancelled)
